2605.29453.title: Forget Less, Generalize More: Unifying Temporal and Structural Adaptation for Dynamic Graphs
2605.29453.paper_summary: Representation learning on dynamic graphs requires capturing complex dependencies that evolve across both time and structure. Existing approaches typically adopt fixed temporal decay schemes or predetermined structural propagation depths, limiting their ability to generalize across graphs with diverse interaction frequencies and topological characteristics. We propose Dual-Scale Retentive Dynamics (DSRD), a unified framework that maintains a retentive representation state encoding both temporal memory and structural context. DSRD introduces two key components: (i) a retentive state with dual-scale adaptation that jointly models temporal dynamics and structural propagation within a single recurrent formulation, and (ii) adaptive decay kernels with learnable time-sensitivity parameters that automatically balance short-term responsiveness and long-term retention based on the underlying interaction patterns. We provide theoretical analysis establishing the equivalence between event-wise parallel aggregation and efficient recurrent state updates, as well as stability and boundedness guarantees for the learned dynamics. Extensive experiments on 14 real-world benchmarks demonstrate that DSRD consistently achieves state-of-the-art performance on both link prediction and node classification tasks, with strong generalization across transductive and inductive settings.
2605.29453.paper_summary_zh: 動態圖上的表示學習需要捕捉隨時間和結構演變的複雜依賴關係。現有的方法通常採用固定的時間衰減方案或預定的結構傳播深度，限制了它們在具有多樣互動頻率和拓撲特徵的圖上進行泛化的能力。我們提出了雙尺度保留動態（DSRD），這是一個統一框架，維持一個同時編碼時間記憶和結構上下文的保留表示狀態。DSRD引入了兩個關鍵組件：（i）具有雙尺度適應的保留狀態，該狀態在單一的遞歸公式中共同建模時間動態和結構傳播，以及（ii）具有可學習時間敏感參數的自適應衰減核，這些核根據潛在的互動模式自動平衡短期反應能力和長期保留。 我們提供了理論分析，確立事件級並行聚合與高效遞歸狀態更新之間的等價性，以及對學習動態的穩定性和有界性保證。在14個現實世界基準上的廣泛實驗表明，DSRD在鏈接預測和節點分類任務上始終達到最先進的性能，並在傳導和誘導設置中具有強大的泛化能力。
2605.29453.author: Qian Chang et.al.
2605.29453.authors: Qian Chang, Ciprian Doru Giurcaneanu, Runsong Jia, Xia Li, Guoping Hu, Xiufeng Cheng, Jinqing Yang, Mengjia Wu, Yi Zhang
